findings.title: APP_DEBUG is enabled
findings.description: APP_DEBUG is set to true. In production, this exposes detailed error messages including stack traces, database queries, and environment variables to end users.
findings.remediation: Set APP_DEBUG=false in your production .env file. Use Laravel's logging system for error tracking instead.

findings.title: APP_ENV is set to 'local'
findings.description: The application environment suggests a non-production configuration. If this is a production server, this may cause debug features to be enabled and performance optimizations to be skipped.
findings.remediation: Set APP_ENV=production on production servers.

findings.title: CORS allows all origins
findings.description: config/cors.php allows requests from any origin ('*'). This permits cross-site data theft if authenticated endpoints return sensitive data.
findings.remediation: Specify allowed origins: 'allowed_origins' => [env('FRONTEND_URL')],

findings.title: [CVE-2025-54068] livewire/livewire@3.5.8 — Livewire is vulnerable to remote command execution during component property update hydration
findings.description: Livewire is vulnerable to remote command execution during component property update hydration
findings.remediation:
Upgrade livewire/livewire to 3.6.4 or later:
  composer require livewire/livewire:3.6.4

findings.title: Route without middleware
findings.description: A route is defined without any middleware. Depending on the route, this may expose endpoints without authentication or rate limiting. Sensitive routes should always have auth or throttle middleware.

findings.title: sha1() used for security purposes
findings.description: SHA-1 is considered weak — practical collision attacks exist. While less broken than MD5, it should not be used for security-sensitive operations like password hashing or token generation.
findings.remediation:
Use SHA-256 or stronger:
  hash('sha256', $data)
For passwords, always use Hash::make().

findings.title: sha1() used for security purposes
findings.description: SHA-1 is considered weak — practical collision attacks exist. While less broken than MD5, it should not be used for security-sensitive operations like password hashing or token generation.
findings.remediation:
Use SHA-256 or stronger:
  hash('sha256', $data)
For passwords, always use Hash::make().

findings.title: dd() call left in code
findings.description: dd() (dump and die) stops execution and dumps variable contents to the browser. Left in production code, it breaks functionality and can expose sensitive data structures, database contents, or environment variables.

findings.title: var_dump() or print_r() in source
findings.description: Native PHP debug functions output raw data to the browser. In production these expose internal variable contents and data structures.
findings.remediation:
Replace with structured logging:
  Log::debug('variable state', ['data' => $data]);

findings.title: var_dump() or print_r() in source
findings.description: Native PHP debug functions output raw data to the browser. In production these expose internal variable contents and data structures.
findings.remediation:
Replace with structured logging:
  Log::debug('variable state', ['data' => $data]);

findings.title: var_dump() or print_r() in source
findings.description: Native PHP debug functions output raw data to the browser. In production these expose internal variable contents and data structures.

Replace with structured logging:
  Log::debug('variable state', ['data' => $data]);

findings.title: Wildcard CORS origin allowed
findings.description: The CORS configuration allows all origins ('*'). This permits any website to make authenticated cross-origin requests to your API, potentially exposing user data through CSRF-like attacks.
findings.remediation:
Specify explicit allowed origins:
  'allowed_origins' => [env('FRONTEND_URL', 'https://myapp.com')],

findings.title: Empty $guarded array on Eloquent model
findings.description: Setting $guarded = [] makes every attribute mass-assignable. An attacker can inject unexpected fields (is_admin, role, email_verified_at) through mass assignment via create() or update().

findings.title: Unescaped Blade output {!! !!}
findings.description: The {!! !!} syntax renders raw HTML without escaping. If the value contains user-controlled data, this creates a stored or reflected XSS vulnerability. Use {{ }} which auto-escapes via htmlspecialchars().
findings.remediation:
Use escaped output:
  {{ $variable }}        — auto-escapes HTML entities
If raw HTML is truly needed, sanitize first:
  {!! clean($variable) !!}   — use HTMLPurifier or similar

findings.title: JavaScript variable from unescaped PHP
findings.description: A PHP variable is interpolated into a JavaScript block without using @json or proper encoding, which can lead to XSS if the value contains user data.
findings.remediation:
Use @json to safely embed PHP data in JavaScript:
  <script>
    var data = @json($data);
  </script>
Or use a data attribute:
  <div data-config="{{ json_encode($config) }}">
